body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10144-z EuCo2Al9 is identified as a metallic spin supersolid with high thermal conductivity, exhibiting coexisting spin orders, strong quantum fluctuations and a giant magnetocaloric effect enabling efficient sub-Kelvin refrigeration.

title: Lasting Lower Rhine–Meuse forager ancestry shaped Bell Beaker expansion

A distinctive population with high hunter-gatherer ancestry persisted 3,000 years later than in most European regions, contributing to later Lower Rhine–…
body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10111-8 A distinctive population with high hunter-gatherer ancestry persisted 3,000 years later than in most European regions, contributing to later Lower Rhine–Meuse Bell Beaker users.

title: Continuous-wave narrow-linewidth vacuum ultraviolet laser source
summary:
Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10107-4

A continuous-wave narrow-linewidth vacuum ultraviolet laser generated using four-wave mixing in cadmium vapour is shown to improve linewidth by five orde…
body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10107-4 A continuous-wave narrow-linewidth vacuum ultraviolet laser generated using four-wave mixing in cadmium vapour is shown to improve linewidth by five orders of magnitude compared with previous single-frequency lasers below 190 nm.

title: Striatum-wide dopamine encodes trajectory errors separated from value
summary:
Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10083-1

Cue-evoked striatal dopamine release in mice encodes bidirectional trajectory errors, spatially and temporally separated from value coding, reflecting th…
body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-025-10083-1 Cue-evoked striatal dopamine release in mice encodes bidirectional trajectory errors, spatially and temporally separated from value coding, reflecting the relationship between the speed and direction of ongoing movement relative to optimal goal trajectories.

title: Sub-second volumetric 3D printing by synthesis of holographic light fields
summary:
Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10114-5

A new method is described that uses a periscope to generate high-speed, high-resolution projections of light fields, enabling sub-second volumetric 3D pr…
body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10114-5 A new method is described that uses a periscope to generate high-speed, high-resolution projections of light fields, enabling sub-second volumetric 3D printing of millimetre-scale objects without rotating the sample.

title: Parity-doublet coherence times in optically trapped polyatomic molecules
summary:
Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10133-2

Optically trapped CaOH molecules in parity-doublet states achieve coherence times exceeding vibrational lifetimes, which are a defining milestone for the…
body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10133-2 Optically trapped CaOH molecules in parity-doublet states achieve coherence times exceeding vibrational lifetimes, which are a defining milestone for the use of polyatomic molecules in quantum science.

title: Sub-part-per-trillion test of the Standard Model with atomic hydrogen
summary:
Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10124-3

Measurement of the 2S–6P transition in cryogenic atomic hydrogen using laser spectroscopy reveals a proton radius value that is 2.5-fold more precise tha…
body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10124-3 Measurement of the 2S–6P transition in cryogenic atomic hydrogen using laser spectroscopy reveals a proton radius value that is 2.5-fold more precise than previous determinations and in excellent agreement with the muonic value, and tests the Standard Model to 0.7 parts per trillion.

body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00174-y Could the discovery of a catalyst in which aluminium shifts easily between oxidation states be the beginning of the end for costly transition-metal catalysts?

title: Babies at nursery shape each other’s microbiomes
summary:
Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00396-0

The study of the gut microbiome of infants in nurseries revealed that peer-to-peer interaction during the first years of life is a prominent route of acq…
body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00396-0 The study of the gut microbiome of infants in nurseries revealed that peer-to-peer interaction during the first years of life is a prominent route of acquisition of microbial strain diversity. The impact of nursery-acquired microorganisms is comparable to that of microorganisms sourced, starting at birth, from family members.

body: Nature, Published online: 11 February 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00440-z Ancient inhabitants of the Rhine–Meuse river delta resisted population shifts that transformed most of Europe — until they helped to catalyse the expansion of ‘Bell Beaker’ culture.
